channel.title: Fork/Join Common Pool on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts
channel.description: Recent content in Fork/Join Common Pool on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts

item.title: Be sure of using Fork/Join common pool; they are daemon threads
item.description: There are two kinds of threads in java- user thread and daemon thread The user threads are priority threads. The user thread dies when it completes its work. So if not terminated by any external means, the thread will live until it finishes its work. On the other hand, daemon threads are created from a user thread and serve the user thread. So a daemon thread may die without completing its work if the user thread dies from which it was created.
